suggestions: Test the system with the GPU mounted directly and no riser cable.
Reseat the GPU and GPU power cables.
Run a GPU stress test after removing the riser.
Test with two RAM sticks only.
Reseat all memory modules.
Try a different RAM kit if available.
Clear CMOS before retesting.
likely_causes: Board or IMC sensitivity with full DIMM population

suggestions: Reseat RAM and test one stick at a time.
Clear CMOS and retest with minimal hardware.
Try another known-good RAM kit if available.
Check whether the issue follows the OS drive.
Test with another drive or fresh Windows install if appropriate.
Update chipset and storage-related drivers.
Check CPU and GPU temperatures under load.
Inspect cooler mount, pump/fan operation, and thermal paste contact.
Test with the side panel open if airflow is questionable.
Check all motherboard and GPU power connections.
Inspect for loose extension cables or adapters.
Test outside the case if there is any concern about a short or mounting issue.
Check power connections and reseat RAM.
Clear CMOS and retest with minimal hardware.
Monitor whether the restart happens at idle, boot, or under load.
Test one RAM stick at a time if memory has not been isolated.
Check whether the freeze follows a specific drive, app, or game.
Watch temps and note whether audio continues or the display cuts out.
Reseat the GPU and power connectors.
Test another display output and cable.
Try the system without the riser cable if one is installed.
Test with another GPU if available.
Bypass the riser cable and test direct GPU mounting.
Reseat the GPU and check PCIe power connections.
Try another known-good display cable and port.
Confirm the drive is detected consistently.
Reseat or swap the drive being used for boot.
Try a known-good OS drive if available.
Clear CMOS and test with minimal hardware only.
Test one RAM stick at a time in the primary slot.
Check CPU seating, cooler pressure, and board power connections.
Test outside the case if a short is possible.
Update or clean reinstall GPU drivers.
Check GPU temps and fan behavior under load.
Test another GPU or bypass the riser if one is installed.
Retest with fewer RAM sticks if possible.
Try another known-good RAM kit.
Check whether the crash is limited to one game or occurs across multiple titles.
likely_causes: Board sensitivity with current memory configuration

suggestions: Check temperatures during normal use and under load.
Check whether the slowdown affects the whole system or one drive/app only.
Test from another drive or fresh Windows install if behavior looks OS-related.
Inspect cooler mount, fan curve, and pump/fan operation.
Check for blocked airflow or heavy dust buildup.
Retest with the side panel open if case airflow is in question.
Test another GPU if available.
Check GPU thermals and fan behavior.
Inspect display cable and alternate outputs.
Test without the riser cable if present.
Reseat the drive and confirm it is detected consistently.
Test another slot, cable, or drive if available.
Check whether the problem follows one specific drive.
Check whether the target drive is detected consistently.
Test installation on another drive.
Reseat NVMe or SATA connections.
Clear any unusual storage configuration and retest.
